title: Job Finding Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Job Finding Assistance 64 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions African American Community Homelessness Issues Seasonal Workers Substance Use Disorder Issues Veterans 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER CLACKAMAS CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 11690 SE 82nd Avenue Happy Valley, OR 97086 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-4623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER O'ROURKE CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. CHANGE CLUB HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a recovery drop-in center with support services for substance addiction, including support groups and an employment center. Additional services include laundry facilities, showers, and snacks. Services are available for individuals at any stage of recovery. 100 SE Cleveland Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Drop-In Center: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Showers and laundry: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm (503) 933-2440 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD OASIS DROP-IN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A collaborative project that provides homeless families priority access to a coordinated set of services offered at multiple locations in the G Street neighborhood. The participating organizations function as a campus, with all services within easy walking distance. Services include: -A safe place to be during the day -Access to laundry, showers, and restroom facilities -A place to prepare and eat simple meals -Help finding affordable housing and employment -Access to additional school and community based resources 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness and at least one child age 17 and younger in their care Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-4pm, Wednesday 8am-6pm, closed holidays Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN BLACKBURN CENTER WORKSOURCE EXPRESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides job search assistance, training information, WorkSource registration, iMatch, online application assistance, and access to a clothing closet for job interviews. Registration also available to local agencies sending participants for Welcome and WIOA. 12121 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 361-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN EMPLOYMENT ACCESS CENTER WORKSOURCE EXPRESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides job search assistance, training information, WorkSource registration, iMatch, online application assistance, and access to a clothing closet for job interviews. Registration also available to local agencies sending participants for Welcome and WIOA. 2 NW 2nd Avenue Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 226-7387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMPASS HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services that include advocacy, assistance navigating public services, meals, and help obtaining employment. Offers education assistance through tutoring and navigating educational resources in the community. The Clubhouse Model of rehabilitation is utilized. Membership is voluntary and without time limits. 37 N Ivy Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with a diagnosed mental illness Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Tours: Monday-Friday 8am-3:30pm Meals: Varies call for information Email (541) 973-2840 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS VA REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals access benefits and services earned through military service and provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) and other government agencies such as healthcare, disability compensation, education, employment, and financial assistance. Provides additional support services like job finding assistance, Medicare information and counseling, transportation to medical appointments, emergency financial grants, support groups, volunteer opportunities, advocacy, and more. 100 SW Main Street Room 212a Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Veterans or dependents of veterans, with a focus on those with disabilities Hours: -National Call Center: 6am-1pm -Local Office Phone: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-3:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon -Local Office Walk in: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-2:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon Email (503) 412-4750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER JOB SEARCH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Maintains a job board for people who are searching for work. 3520 Frontage Road Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 271-9700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 12685 SW 4th Street Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-5pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday 2pm-5pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE THURSDAY NIGHT DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 494 E Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RIVERFRONT SCHOOL AND CAREER CENTER RIVER FRONT SCHOOL AND CAREER CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accredited alternative school offering educational and vocational training. Services include comprehensive career and basic skill assessment, computer skills instruction, work readiness and job search assistance, and job training programs. Assists with transportation, job search clothing, child care, and GED testing. 1666 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: At-risk and out-of-school youth ages 14-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 302-2554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID ROGUE FOUNDATION MOBILE NAVIGATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outreach assistance with housing and job search help, identification assistance (ID and Social Security cards), replacement birth certificate help with application and fee, general mail delivery, credit report requests, and SNAP and OHP application assistance. Also helps to connect people to services for savings, spending, educational plans, and life skills. Mobile Navigators meet clients in public spaces that work best for the client. PO Box 1972 Grants Pass, OR 97528 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 295-5706 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT PROGRAM SUPPORTED EMPLOYMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists individuals needing help finding competitive employment. After a job placement is completed, the employee and the employer are provided with support for as long as required. 3425 13TH Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focuses on individuals recovering from mental or physical health conditions that impact employment. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 523-7400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST SEASONAL WORKERS ASSOCIATION 11 POINT BENEFIT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food and clothing, preventive medical care, limited non-emergency dental, legal advice, job referrals, advocacy, and information and referral. Services are prioritized for members. Volunteers are provided with organizational skills and advocacy methods. 203 N Oakdale Avenue Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Seasonal and other low-income workers. Membership required. Services for members and their families are benefits of membership. Hours: 7 days per week 9am-9pm, including holidays Email (541) 773-6811 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC THE HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a recovery-oriented drop-in center where participants can sit, have a snack, and connect with others. Services include case management and information and referral to community resources, job finding assistance and employment support. Offers a variety of support groups that cover topics such as wellness, Medication Assisted Treatment (MAT) Education, and making connections. Assist with low-barrier access to treatment services and provides peer support for substance-related issues. Substance use and mental health assessments can be done by telehealth. Provides referrals for MAT. Distributes naloxone and offers education in partnership with other agencies. 404 SW 5th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-5pm, Wednesday 9am-12:30pm and 3pm-5pm (541) 916-5723 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF MULTNOMAH COUNTY EAST JOBS OFFICE, CHILD WELFARE, SELF-SUFFICIENCY, FAMILY STABILITY AND EMPLOYMENT, AND TEEN PARENT SELF-SUFFICIENCY JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Steps to Success, a partnership between ODHS and Portland Community College, is an integrated program that provides individuals with the tools to become self-sufficient. This program provides intensive assistance, job search skills and training, assistance to receive a GED or ABE, counseling services, alcohol/drug programs, and employment services. JOBS is the state's employment and training program for people receiving public assistance. One segment of the JOBS program, known as JOBS Plus, places TANF recipients in limited-duration training positions with private employers. 11826 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed major holidays (971) 673-0909 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF MULTNOMAH COUNTY GRESHAM SELF-SUFFICIENCY JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF JOBS is the state's employment and training program for people receiving public assistance. One segment of the JOBS program, known as JOBS Plus, places TANF recipients in limited-duration training positions with private employers. 635 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 102 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed major holidays (503) 673-8500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OF JACKSON AND JOSEPHINE COUNTY NATIONAL FARMWORKER JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services to assist farmworkers (seasonal or migrant) in obtaining permanent and higher-paying employment opportunities. English as a second language, GED/High school equivalency test instruction, career services, classes, training, and on-the-job experiences, skills training and certifications for farmworkers, support with the job application and search process, career counseling, financial assistance for college, and education and career services for agricultural workers and their dependents. Depending on location and funding availability, specific classes, training opportunities, and financial assistance will vary. 820 S Central Avenue Suite A Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are an agricultural worker, the dependent, or spouse of an agricultural worker who meet federal income guidelines and are authorized to in the United States. Must be enrolled or in the process of enrolling at an accredited community college, vocational school/program, apprenticeship, or 4-year university. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 881-1491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON ROHINGYA MYANMAR MUSLIM SOCIETY EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers employment and career services including job placement assistance, resume building, interview preparation, and career counseling. Also provides support in navigating the local job market, offering guidance on workers rights and workplace culture. 2410 SE 121st Avenue Suite #208 Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Must meet one of the following: -Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers -Low-income individuals and families residing in Oregon -Individuals with limited English proficiency -Families experiencing hardship, displacement, or housing instability -Youth, adults, and elders across all ages -BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, Persons of Color) -Rohingya and Myanmar Community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 489-5179 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Independent Living Program (ILP) helps youth who are, or were, in foster care to become self-sufficient adults. It gives an opportunity to learn valuable skills necessary to make a successful transition from state and Tribal custody to living on their own in the community. Services include access to case management and funds for continuing education and assistance with housing. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E67 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or former foster children ages 16-20 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 945-6969 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SELF SUFFICIENCY ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE SNAP TRAINING AND EMPLOYMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists SNAP recipients to gain skills, training, work experience, and employment to increase their ability to obtain living-wage employment. May be able to cover the cost of training, textbooks, gas, bus tickets, housing and utility costs, and work clothes. 500 Summer Street NE E48 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: The STEP program is for people in Oregon who: -Are receiving SNAP benefits, -Are at least 16 years old, and -Are not receiving TANF benefits. The ABAWD program is for people in Oregon who: -Ages 18-49 -No children under 18 years old on their SNAP case -Do not meet an exemption or live in an exempt county Hours: Monday- Friday 8am-5pm (833) 947-1964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Employment program assists with job coaching and job placement, resume building, training, and educational costs. PATHWAYS TO SUCCESS: Helps low-income community members obtain living wage employment. LIVING SOLUTIONS: This program helps homeless and very low-income people who want to work but are not well suited for traditional employment programs. Living Solutions offers job training and job placement assistance with the goal of placing participants in living wage jobs. Services include basic education, skills training, and follow-up job coaching. COMMUNITY WORKS: Community Works is a consortium which provides a multi-cultural and individualized approach to helping low income parents to successfully transition off TANF and back into the workforce. DEPARTMENT OF COMMUNITY JUSTICE WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM: This program provides services to individuals who have been identified by the court system. Partners work together to ensure participants have the range of career coaching, training, housing and alcohol and drug treatment services they need. 10550 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 548-0200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE OUR JUST FUTURE ROCKWOOD BUILDING EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Employment program assists with job coaching and job placement, resume building, training, and educational costs. PATHWAYS TO SUCCESS: Helps low-income community members obtain living wage employment. LIVING SOLUTIONS: This program helps homeless and very low-income people who want to work but are not well suited for traditional employment programs. Living Solutions offers job training and job placement assistance with the goal of placing participants in living wage jobs. Services include basic education, skills training, and follow-up job coaching. COMMUNITY WORKS: Community Works is a consortium which provides a multi-cultural and individualized approach to helping low income parents to successfully transition off TANF and back into the workforce. DEPARTMENT OF COMMUNITY JUSTICE WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM: This program provides services to individuals who have been identified by the court system.
